name: Social Security
description: Monthly benefits for retirees, disabled workers, and survivors. The largest single federal expenditure, serving over 72 million Americans.
spendingItems: Survivor benefits to 5.8M widows, widowers, and children
SSA administrative operations across 1,200+ field offices
IT systems for benefit calculations and claims processing

fraudEstimate.source: SSA Office of Inspector General FY2023 Annual Report
examples: Disability fraud — working while collecting SSDI (~$4B estimated)
Payments to deceased beneficiaries ($170M annually recovered)
Payments to prisoners who should be suspended ($65M/year)
Identity theft and fraudulent applications ($800M+)

subCategories.name: Old Age & Survivors Insurance (OASI)
subCategories.description: Retirement and survivor benefits — the core Social Security program, funded by FICA payroll taxes.
spendingItems: Monthly retirement checks to 57.8M retirees and dependents
Survivor benefits to widows/widowers and minor children
Spousal benefits (up to 50% of worker's benefit)

examples: Payments to unreported deceased ($1.1B)
Concurrent benefit fraud

subCategories.name: Disability Insurance (SSDI)
subCategories.lead: Office of Disability Adjudication and Review
subCategories.description: Monthly payments to workers who become disabled before retirement age and cannot perform substantial work.
spendingItems: Monthly payments averaging $1,489 to 8.7M disabled workers
Benefits to disabled workers' families and dependents
Continuing disability reviews (CDRs) — evaluating ongoing eligibility
Vocational rehabilitation services and work incentive programs

examples: Working while claiming total disability
Fraudulent medical evidence from 'disability mills'
Attorney fraud in claims filing (Operation Hidden Treasure)
Examiners approving improper claims — 'Huntington, WV scandal' cost $2.5B+

subCategories.name: SSA Administration
subCategories.lead: Deputy Commissioner for Budget, Finance & Management
subCategories.description: Operating costs for SSA's 60,000 employees, field offices, and technology systems.
spendingItems: IT modernization of legacy COBOL systems (60+ years old)

examples: IT contractor overcharges
Improper vendor payments

name: Medicare
description: Federal health insurance for 65+ Americans and certain disabled individuals. Covers hospital, outpatient, and prescription drug costs.
leadTitle: Deputy Administrator & Director, Center for Medicare, CMS
spendingItems: Hospital inpatient care (Part A) for 34M+ beneficiaries
Outpatient doctor visits, labs, durable equipment (Part B)
Medicare Advantage private plan subsidies (Part C)
Graduate medical education payments to teaching hospitals
Disproportionate share payments to safety-net hospitals

fraudEstimate.source: CMS Improper Payment Report FY2023; HHS OIG
examples: Billing for services never rendered (~$28B estimated annually)
Upcoding — billing for more expensive procedures than performed
Phantom billing by ghost clinics and DME suppliers
COVID-19 telehealth fraud schemes ($2B+ charged)
Lab test kickback schemes ($6B Operation Brace Yourself)

subCategories.name: Medicare Advantage (Part C)
subCategories.description: Payments to private insurance companies to provide Medicare benefits to enrolled seniors.
spendingItems: Capitated payments to 500+ approved private insurers
Risk adjustment payments based on beneficiary health status
Quality bonus payments to high-rated plans

fraudEstimate.source: HHS OIG; CMS Risk Adjustment Audits
examples: Risk score inflation — insurers exaggerating patient illness to get higher payments
Chart-chase audits finding $4.2B overbilling by UnitedHealth, Humana, others
Phantom diagnoses added without supporting medical records

subCategories.name: Medicare Part A — Hospital Insurance
subCategories.description: Covers inpatient hospital stays, skilled nursing facilities, hospice, and some home health care.
spendingItems: Hospice care for terminally ill patients

examples: Unnecessary inpatient admissions when outpatient would suffice
Hospice fraud — enrolling non-terminal patients for higher reimbursement
SNF billing for patients not meeting 3-day hospital requirement

subCategories.name: Medicare Part B — Medical Insurance
subCategories.description: Covers outpatient care, doctor visits, preventive services, lab tests, and durable medical equipment.
spendingItems: Physician and surgeon fees for office and outpatient visits
Lab tests, imaging, and diagnostic services
Preventive care: annual wellness visits, screenings, vaccines

fraudEstimate.source: CMS; DOJ Healthcare Fraud Strike Force data
examples: Phantom billing for equipment never delivered ($2B+ annually)
Back brace and DME kickback schemes ($6B Operation Brace Yourself)
Upcoded evaluation codes (billing 99215 for routine visits)

subCategories.name: Medicare Part D — Prescription Drugs
subCategories.description: Prescription drug coverage provided through private plan sponsors, subsidized by Medicare.
spendingItems: Subsidies to Part D plan sponsors (PDPs and MA-PD plans)
Low-income subsidy (Extra Help) for eligible beneficiaries

examples: Pharmacy billing for drugs not dispensed
Pill mill prescribers enabling opioid diversion
Duplicate billing across multiple Part D plans

name: Medicaid & CHIP
description: Health coverage for 90M+ low-income Americans including children, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities. Jointly funded by federal and state governments.
leadTitle: Deputy Administrator & Director, Center for Medicaid and CHIP, CMS
spendingItems: Federal matching funds to all 50 states + DC for Medicaid
Long-term care for elderly and disabled in nursing facilities
Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) for uninsured children
ACA expansion coverage for low-income adults
Behavioral health and substance use disorder treatment

fraudEstimate.source: CMS FY2023 Improper Payment Rate; HHS OIG
examples: Enrollees who don't meet income/eligibility requirements
Personal care fraud — billing for services not rendered ($8B+ annually)
Provider billing for deceased Medicaid recipients
Phantom provider schemes — billing for non-existent services
MCO (managed care) capitation overpayments to insurers ($25B est.)

subCategories.name: Medicaid Federal Match (FMAP)
subCategories.lead: State Medicaid Agencies (Federal match via CMS)
subCategories.leadTitle: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services + All 50 States
subCategories.description: Federal share of Medicaid spending, matched to state spending. Federal match ranges from 50% to 83% depending on state income levels.
spendingItems: Acute care — doctor visits, ER, hospital inpatient
Long-term services and supports (nursing homes, home care)
Behavioral health and mental health services

fraudEstimate.source: CMS PERM measurement; HHS OIG FY2023
examples: Home health and personal care aides billing for no-shows
MCO risk score manipulation
Eligibility fraud — earning over limits while enrolled

subCategories.name: Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP)
subCategories.description: Low-cost health coverage for children in families who earn too much to qualify for Medicaid but can't afford private insurance.
spendingItems: Doctor visits and well-child care for 7M+ children

examples: Eligibility errors
Provider billing irregularities

name: National Defense
description: Military operations, personnel, weapons systems, and national security. Includes the Department of Defense, nuclear weapons programs, and intelligence agencies.
spendingItems: Pay and benefits for 1.3M active duty military personnel
Operations and maintenance of military equipment and bases
Procurement of aircraft, ships, vehicles, and weapons
Research and development of next-generation systems
Defense intelligence: NSA, DIA, NRO, NGA

fraudEstimate.source: DoD OIG Annual Report FY2023; GAO Defense Reports
examples: Contractor overbilling and cost mischarging ($2B+ detected annually)
DoD has NEVER passed a full financial audit (6 failed attempts since 2018)
F-35 program: $1.7T lifetime cost, years behind schedule, persistent defects
Spare parts price gouging — Pentagon paid $14,000 for a $50 part
Housing allowance fraud by service members
Counterfeit parts in military equipment supply chains

subCategories.name: Military Personnel
subCategories.lead: Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel & Readiness
subCategories.description: Pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for all active-duty military members.
spendingItems: Base pay for 1.3M active duty members

examples: BAH (housing allowance) fraud — falsifying dependents
Ghost pay and AWOL collection

subCategories.name: Operations & Maintenance
subCategories.description: Day-to-day costs of running the military: training, equipment maintenance, base operations, and logistics.

fraudEstimate.source: DoD OIG; POGO defense contractor database
examples: Contractor labor mischarging ($500M+ per year detected)
Phantom services billed on LOGCAP contracts in Afghanistan
Defective pricing by major contractors (DCAA audits)

subCategories.name: Procurement
subCategories.lead: Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition
subCategories.description: Buying weapons systems, aircraft, ships, vehicles, ammunition, and other military equipment.

fraudEstimate.source: DoD OIG; GAO defense acquisition reports
examples: F-35 program: $13B in cost overruns since inception
LCS (Littoral Combat Ship): $5B over budget, program cancelled
Sole-source contracts enabling price gouging by Lockheed, Raytheon, Boeing

subCategories.name: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation
subCategories.lead: Under Secretary of Defense for Research & Engineering
subCategories.description: Funding for basic research through weapons system development and testing.
spendingItems: Basic and applied defense research at universities and labs
Advanced technology development (AI, hypersonics, directed energy)
System development and demonstration for new weapons

examples: Cost overruns on developmental programs ($2B+ per year)
Contractor IP theft and misappropriation
Duplication of R&D across services

subCategories.name: Nuclear Weapons & Defense (NNSA)
subCategories.description: Nuclear weapons maintenance, modernization, and nonproliferation programs under the Department of Energy.
spendingItems: Maintenance and modernization of the U.S. nuclear stockpile
Nuclear weapons labs: Sandia, Los Alamos, Lawrence Livermore
Naval reactor design for submarines and carriers
Environmental cleanup at former nuclear weapons sites

examples: Plutonium facility (CMRR) cancelled after $700M spent
Contractor mismanagement at DOE sites costing billions in overruns

subCategories.name: Defense Intelligence Agencies
subCategories.leadTitle: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
subCategories.description: Classified programs for NSA signals intelligence, NRO satellite reconnaissance, NGA geospatial analysis, and DIA military intelligence.
spendingItems: NSA — global signals intelligence and cybersecurity
Classified cyber offensive and defensive operations

fraudEstimate.source: ICIG; classified budget leak data (Snowden documents)
examples: Contractor security clearance fraud (e.g., NavySea background check scandal)
Cost overruns on classified satellite programs

name: Interest on Debt
description: Mandatory payments on the $33.9 trillion national debt. Now exceeds the entire defense budget. Will continue growing as debt and interest rates remain elevated.
spendingItems: Interest on Treasury bonds held by the public ($550B)
Interest on intragovernmental debt (Social Security Trust Fund, etc.) ($109B)
Payments to foreign debt holders — China ($800B), Japan ($1.1T)
Payments to Federal Reserve, mutual funds, pension funds, individuals

fraudEstimate.source: N/A — debt service is a contractual obligation with no fraud component
examples: Note: The waste here is political, not fraudulent — decades of deficit spending created this mandatory expense
Every dollar of interest is money NOT spent on services Americans use

subCategories.name: Interest to Public Creditors
subCategories.description: Interest paid to individuals, corporations, foreign governments, and the Federal Reserve who hold U.S. Treasury securities.

subCategories.name: Intragovernmental Interest
subCategories.description: Interest paid to federal trust funds that have lent money to the general fund, primarily Social Security and Medicare.
spendingItems: Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund interest (~$7B)
Military and civil service retirement trust funds
Highway, airport, and other special-purpose fund interest

name: Income Security
description: Safety net programs providing food, housing, and income support to low-income Americans, disabled individuals, and families in need.
leadTitle: Multiple Departments: HHS, USDA, HUD, Treasury
spendingItems: Supplemental Security Income (SSI) to disabled/elderly poor
Earned Income Tax Credit refundable payments

fraudEstimate.source: OMB Improper Payments Report FY2023; USDA OIG; GAO
examples: EITC improper payments: $17.4B annually (31% error/fraud rate — IRS estimate)
Unemployment fraud during COVID: $135B paid to ineligible claimants (DOL estimate)
SNAP trafficking — retailers exchanging EBT for cash at discount
SSI recipients exceeding asset limits (~$4B)
Rental assistance fraud — unreported income from tenants and landlords

subCategories.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
subCategories.leadTitle: Administrator, Food and Nutrition Service, USDA
subCategories.description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program provides monthly benefits via EBT card for food purchases to 41M low-income Americans.
spendingItems: Employment and training programs for recipients

fraudEstimate.source: USDA FNS Payment Integrity Report FY2023
examples: SNAP trafficking (exchanging benefits for cash) — $1.3B/year detected
Eligibility fraud — unreported income or assets
Retailer fraud — phantom transactions

subCategories.name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
subCategories.description: Refundable tax credit for low-to-moderate income workers. One of the largest anti-poverty programs, delivering $77B in refundable credits.
spendingItems: Maximum credit of $7,430 for families with 3+ children

examples: Claiming children who don't qualify or don't exist
Inflated self-employment income to maximize credit
Paid preparer fraud — filing false EITC claims for kickbacks

subCategories.name: Federal Housing Assistance
subCategories.description: Section 8 housing vouchers, public housing, and project-based rental assistance helping low-income families afford decent housing.
spendingItems: Public housing operating and capital funds

examples: Unreported income by tenants ($1.5B+)
Fraudulent landlord billing for uninspected units
Ineligible recipients remaining on program after income increases

subCategories.name: Unemployment Insurance
subCategories.description: Federal-state partnership providing temporary income replacement to workers who lose jobs through no fault of their own.
spendingItems: Extended benefits federal share during high unemployment
Federal Unemployment Tax (FUTA) administrative grants to states
UI modernization and fraud prevention grants

examples: COVID-era fraud: $135B total paid to ineligible claimants (2020-2023)
Organized fraud rings using stolen identities (Operation Heist)
Continued work while claiming UI
State systems exploited due to outdated COBOL technology

subCategories.name: Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
subCategories.description: Monthly payments to disabled adults and children and elderly (65+) with very limited income and resources.

examples: Concealed assets (savings, property) exceeding $2,000 limit
Unreported wages or other income
Failure to report household composition changes

name: Veterans' Affairs
description: Healthcare, education, and benefits for 19M U.S. veterans. VA operates the nation's largest integrated healthcare system.
spendingItems: VA healthcare for 9M enrolled veterans (171 medical centers)
Pension for wartime veterans with low income

fraudEstimate.source: VA OIG Annual Report FY2023; GAO
examples: Disability rating fraud — exaggerating or fabricating conditions
GI Bill fraud — schools enrolling unqualified veterans for tuition
Pension fraud — concealing assets or income ($400M+ annually)
Community Care billing irregularities ($1B+ in questioned costs)

subCategories.name: Disability Compensation
subCategories.description: Monthly tax-free payments to veterans for service-connected disabilities. Average payment: $1,900/month.
spendingItems: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) for survivors
Special monthly compensation for severe disabilities

examples: Fraudulent disability claims — fabricating or exaggerating injuries
Veterans concealing VA employment that should reduce benefits
C&P exam manipulation

subCategories.name: VA Healthcare System
subCategories.description: Medical care for 9M enrolled veterans at 171 VA Medical Centers and 1,100+ outpatient clinics nationwide.
spendingItems: Inpatient hospital care at VA medical centers
Community Care Network — private sector care for veterans

fraudEstimate.source: VA OIG FY2023; Community Care audits
examples: Community Care overbilling by private providers ($800M+ questioned)
Supply chain and procurement irregularities
Employee time and attendance fraud

subCategories.name: GI Bill & Education
subCategories.description: Education and training benefits under Chapter 33 (Post-9/11 GI Bill) and other programs covering tuition, housing, and supplies.
spendingItems: Tuition and fees paid directly to schools

examples: For-profit school fraud — ITT Tech, Corinthian Colleges misuse
Schools misreporting enrollment to collect housing allowances
GI Bill used at schools later sanctioned or closed

name: Education
description: Federal student aid, K-12 grants, and education research. The $1.7T student loan portfolio is the government's largest financial asset.
spendingItems: Federal student loan disbursements and income-driven repayment
Title I grants to high-poverty K-12 schools

fraudEstimate.source: ED OIG FY2023; GAO Student Aid Reports
examples: Student loan fraud — fake enrollment and identity theft ($3B+ annually)
Pell Grant fraud — students not attending classes
For-profit school fraud (Corinthian, ITT, DeVry settlements)
Financial aid eligibility errors costing $4B+

subCategories.name: Federal Student Aid (Loans & Pell)
subCategories.leadTitle: Chief Operating Officer, Federal Student Aid
subCategories.description: Student loans, Pell Grants, and work-study funding for higher education, managed by Federal Student Aid (FSA).
spendingItems: Pell Grants to 6.7M students (~$7,395 max award)

fraudEstimate.source: ED OIG; FSA Improper Payments FY2023
examples: Fraud mills submitting fake FAFSA applications
Pell Grant eligibility fraud ($1.5B+)
Servicer overcharging and improper capitalization of interest

subCategories.name: K-12 Education Grants
subCategories.leadTitle: Assistant Secretary for Elementary & Secondary Education
subCategories.description: Title I, IDEA special education, and other formula grants flowing to all U.S. school districts.
spendingItems: Title I grants to 56,000 high-poverty schools
IDEA special education grants to states

fraudEstimate.source: ED OIG; GAO K-12 Oversight Reports
examples: Grant funds used for non-educational purchases
Misreported student counts for Title I allocation
Improper subcontracting of federal education services

subCategories.name: Head Start & Early Childhood
subCategories.description: Comprehensive early childhood development for low-income children under 5, including education, health, nutrition, and family services.
spendingItems: Early Head Start for infants and toddlers
Child care and development block grants
Home visiting programs for at-risk families

examples: Grantee financial mismanagement
Enrollment inflation

name: Transportation & Infrastructure
description: Highways, bridges, airports, rail, and public transit. Significantly boosted by the $1.2T Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (2021).
spendingItems: FAA — air traffic control, airport safety, airports
Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act implementation

fraudEstimate.source: DOT OIG FY2023; GAO Infrastructure Reports
examples: Highway contract fraud — substandard materials, inflated bids ($1B+ annually)
DBE (Disadvantaged Business Enterprise) front company fraud
Davis-Bacon wage fraud on federally funded projects
FAA contractor overcharges on NextGen air traffic system

subCategories.name: Federal Highway Administration
subCategories.description: Funds construction and maintenance of the Interstate Highway System and other federal-aid roads through grants to states.
spendingItems: State and local road formula grants

fraudEstimate.source: FHWA OIG; FBI highway fraud statistics
examples: Bid rigging on highway contracts (DOJ prosecutions)
DBE fraud — fake minority-owned businesses
Substandard materials misrepresented on bridge projects

subCategories.name: Federal Aviation Administration
subCategories.description: Regulates civil aviation safety, manages the National Airspace System, and funds airport infrastructure.
spendingItems: Air Traffic Organization — 14,000 controllers managing 45,000 daily flights
Drone and urban air mobility integration

examples: Boeing 737 MAX certification failures — inadequate oversight cost 346 lives
Airport grant fund misuse by local authorities
Aviation maintenance fraud — falsified repair records

subCategories.name: Federal Transit Administration
subCategories.description: Capital and operating grants for buses, subways, light rail, and commuter rail systems nationwide.
spendingItems: Capital investment grants (New Starts) for major transit projects
Urbanized area formula grants for operating costs
State of good repair — fixing aging transit infrastructure

examples: Procurement irregularities at transit agencies
Buy America waiver abuse

name: Health Research & Public Health
description: NIH research grants, CDC operations, FDA oversight, and other public health programs.
spendingItems: NIH research grants to universities and labs ($48B)
CDC disease surveillance and prevention programs
FDA food and drug safety regulation
HRSA community health centers and rural health
SAMHSA mental health and addiction treatment grants

fraudEstimate.source: HHS OIG; NIH OIG Research Integrity Reports
examples: NIH grant fraud — fabricated data and duplicate billing ($500M+ per year)
Community health center billing fraud
COVID-19 HRSA uninsured fund fraud ($2.5B overpayments)
Research misconduct — data falsification costing grant resources

subCategories.name: National Institutes of Health (NIH)
subCategories.description: World's largest public funder of biomedical research — supporting 300,000+ researchers at 2,500+ institutions.
spendingItems: Extramural research grants (80% of budget) to universities and medical schools
Intramural research at NIH Bethesda campus
NHLBI ($4B) — heart, lung, and blood diseases

fraudEstimate.source: NIH OIG; HHS OIG Research Integrity
examples: Researchers billing time on multiple conflicting grants
Foreign influence — undisclosed Chinese funding (DOJ China Initiative)
Fabricated data in published studies leading to retracted grants

subCategories.name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
subCategories.description: Protects Americans from health threats. Coordinates disease surveillance, immunization programs, and emergency response.
spendingItems: Infectious disease surveillance and outbreak response
Public health emergency preparedness grants to states

examples: COVID-19 PPE procurement fraud ($1.8B in contracts with fraud indicators)
Grant recipient misuse of funds by state health departments

subCategories.name: Food and Drug Administration
subCategories.description: Regulates food safety, drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, and tobacco — overseeing $2.8T in consumer products annually.
spendingItems: Drug approval review (user fee funded + appropriations)

examples: Fraudulent foreign drug manufacturing records submitted for approval
Clinical trial data manipulation by pharmaceutical companies

name: International Affairs
description: Foreign aid, diplomatic missions, international development, and global security assistance. Less than 1% of the total federal budget.

examples: Foreign partner government corruption siphoning USAID funds
Contractor fraud in conflict zones (Afghanistan: $19B in reconstruction waste)
Duplicate billing by international implementing partners
Embassy construction cost overruns ($100M+ per project)

subCategories.name: USAID Development Assistance
subCategories.description: U.S. Agency for International Development funds economic development, democracy, global health, and disaster relief worldwide.
spendingItems: Global health: HIV/AIDS (PEPFAR $6.9B), malaria, TB programs
Food for Peace and humanitarian assistance
Economic growth and trade capacity building

fraudEstimate.source: USAID OIG; Special Inspector General for Afghanistan
examples: Partner organization fraud in high-risk countries
Afghanistan reconstruction: $19B in waste, fraud and abuse (SIGAR)
Phantom beneficiary counts inflating program metrics

subCategories.name: State Department Operations
subCategories.description: Funding for U.S. embassies, consulates, and diplomatic missions worldwide — America's first line of foreign policy.
spendingItems: Diplomatic security and Marine security guard program

examples: Construction contractor fraud
Visa fraud enabling criminal networks

subCategories.name: Military Security Assistance
subCategories.lead: Under Secretary of State for Arms Control
subCategories.description: Foreign Military Financing (FMF) and other security assistance programs to allied and partner nations.

fraudEstimate.source: DoD OIG; SIGAR; Ukraine aid oversight reports
examples: Weapons diversion risk in conflict zones
End-use monitoring failures — equipment reaching unintended actors

name: Homeland Security
description: Border security, immigration enforcement, airport security, FEMA disaster response, and Coast Guard operations.
spendingItems: Customs and Border Protection — 700-mile wall, ports of entry
TSA — airport security screening at 440+ airports
FEMA disaster response and mitigation grants
Secret Service — presidential protection and financial crimes
Coast Guard maritime safety and interdiction
CISA — cybersecurity of federal networks and critical infrastructure

examples: FEMA disaster fraud — false addresses and fictitious damage claims ($5B+ in COVID/Ida/Harvey)
Border barrier contract fraud — inflated costs and kickbacks
ICE detention contractor billing for unperformed services
TSA equipment procurement fraud

subCategories.name: FEMA — Emergency Management
subCategories.description: Federal Emergency Management Agency coordinates disaster response, mitigation grants, and flood insurance programs.
spendingItems: Individual assistance — disaster survivor housing and cash grants
Public assistance — rebuilding government infrastructure post-disaster

examples: Hurricane Ida, Harvey, Maria: $1B+ in fraudulent individual assistance claims
COVID-19 FEMA funeral assistance fraud: $400M+
Contractors submitting fraudulent debris removal tonnage
Duplicate applications using multiple addresses

subCategories.name: Customs & Border Protection
subCategories.description: Secures U.S. borders against illegal entry of people and contraband while facilitating lawful trade and travel.
spendingItems: 20,000+ Border Patrol agents along southern and northern borders
Port of entry operations — $900B in daily trade
Automated targeting systems for cargo screening

examples: Agent corruption — facilitating drug smuggling for cartels
Border wall contract fraud and cost inflation
CBP equipment procurement overcharges

subCategories.name: Transportation Security Administration
subCategories.description: Protects the nation's transportation systems through airport security screening and surface transportation security.
spendingItems: Airport passenger and baggage screening (56,000 officers)
Advanced imaging technology (AIT scanners) and equipment
PreCheck and Global Entry trusted traveler programs

examples: Equipment procurement fraud — security scanner overbilling
Officer misconduct and theft at checkpoints ($30M+ annually)
PreCheck vetting contract irregularities

subCategories.name: U.S. Coast Guard
subCategories.description: Maritime law enforcement, search and rescue, aids to navigation, and port security — the only armed service in DHS.
spendingItems: Drug interdiction and migrant interdiction at sea
Icebreaking operations in Arctic and Antarctic

examples: Cutter maintenance contract overcharges
Equipment procurement irregularities

name: Housing & Urban Development
description: Federal housing assistance, community development grants, and fair housing enforcement. Serves 4.8M assisted households.
leadTitle: Acting Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
spendingItems: Public housing operating and capital fund

examples: Tenant income fraud — underreporting earnings to maintain vouchers
Landlord rent inflation beyond fair market value
CDBG grant misuse by local governments
Mortgage fraud enabled by FHA programs

subCategories.name: Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8)
subCategories.lead: Office of Public and Indian Housing
subCategories.description: Vouchers enabling 2.3M low-income families to rent in the private market, with HUD paying the difference between 30% of income and fair market rent.
spendingItems: HAP (housing assistance payments) to private landlords
Administrative fees to 2,400+ Public Housing Authorities

examples: Unreported income — tenants hiding wages/self-employment
Undetected household members (ineligible individuals)
Landlord side payments from tenants (cash back from voucher)

subCategories.name: Community Development Block Grants
subCategories.lead: Office of Community Planning and Development
subCategories.description: Formula grants to states and cities for community development, affordable housing, and economic opportunity — flexible local use.
spendingItems: Economic development loans to small businesses

examples: Grantees spending on ineligible activities
Duplication of benefits in disaster recovery
Contractor kickbacks in local CDBG programs

name: Agriculture & Food Safety
description: Farm support programs, rural development, food safety inspection, and conservation. Note: SNAP and child nutrition are counted under Income Security.
spendingItems: Agricultural risk coverage and price loss coverage (farm payments)
Food safety inspection (FSIS) of meat and poultry

fraudEstimate.source: USDA OIG FY2023; GAO Farm Subsidy Reports
examples: Farm subsidy fraud — payments to non-farmers and urban dwellers ($695M identified)
Crop insurance fraud — inflating losses or misrepresenting yields
Rural housing program fraud — misrepresented income eligibility
Conservation payment fraud — claiming credit for non-existent practices

subCategories.name: Farm Support & Commodity Programs
subCategories.description: Direct payments, marketing loans, and price support for corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton, dairy, and other commodities.
spendingItems: Agricultural Risk Coverage (ARC) and Price Loss Coverage (PLC) payments

examples: Payments to deceased farmers ($10M+ annually)
Non-farming entities receiving farm subsidies
Income limit circumvention through entity structuring

subCategories.name: Crop Insurance Subsidies
subCategories.description: Federal subsidy of premiums for crop insurance policies protecting farmers against yield and revenue losses.
spendingItems: Premium subsidy covering 62% of farmers' insurance costs
Reinsurance to 15 approved private crop insurers

examples: False claims — reporting crop losses that didn't occur
Agent fraud — inflating production records for higher payouts
Intentional destruction of crops to collect insurance

name: Energy & Environment
description: Department of Energy programs including nuclear security, clean energy R&D, and grid modernization. Also includes EPA environmental programs.
spendingItems: Nuclear weapons stockpile stewardship (NNSA) — counted in Defense
Clean energy R&D: solar, wind, batteries, hydrogen
Loan programs for clean energy projects ($400B portfolio)
EPA clean water, clean air, and Superfund programs
Environmental cleanup of legacy DOE nuclear sites

examples: Clean energy loan fraud — Solyndra ($535M loss) and similar failures
IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) clean energy credit fraud risk (IRS flagging)
Superfund contractor overcharges
Renewable energy tax credit schemes ($1B+ in fraudulent claims)

subCategories.name: Clean Energy R&D & Deployment
subCategories.description: Research, development, and deployment of solar, wind, batteries, hydrogen, nuclear, and other clean energy technologies.
spendingItems: Office of Energy Efficiency & Renewable Energy (EERE)
DOE loan programs (LPO) — guarantees for innovative projects

fraudEstimate.source: DOE OIG; GAO Clean Energy Reports
examples: Loan Program Office defaults (Solyndra, others)
Grant recipient misuse of funds

subCategories.name: EPA — Environmental Protection
subCategories.description: Enforces environmental laws, funds clean water infrastructure, and operates Superfund toxic site cleanup.
spendingItems: Climate and environmental justice grants (new in IRA)

examples: Superfund contractor overcharges and inflated labor rates
State revolving fund diversion to ineligible projects
Environmental justice grant fraud (emerging issue)

subCategories.name: Environmental Cleanup (DOE Legacy Sites)
subCategories.description: Cleanup of radioactive and chemical contamination at former nuclear weapons sites — a 75-year, $500B+ project.
spendingItems: Hanford Site cleanup (Washington) — largest nuclear cleanup
Savannah River Site (SC) tank waste treatment

examples: Bechtel/AECOM overcharges on Hanford Waste Treatment Plant ($2B+ over budget)
Contractor labor mischarging and fee fraud
Cost-plus contract abuse enabling runaway project costs

name: Science & Technology
description: NASA space exploration, NSF research grants, NOAA weather and climate programs, and NIST standards.
leadTitle: Director, Office of Science & Technology Policy
spendingItems: NASA — Artemis moon program, Mars exploration, James Webb Telescope
NSF — basic research grants across all scientific disciplines
NOAA — weather forecasting, climate research, fisheries
OSTP — federal science and technology policy

examples: NASA contractor overcharges (Boeing SLS: $2.8B over budget)
NSF grant fraud — researchers double-billing or fabricating results
NOAA contract irregularities

subCategories.name: NASA
subCategories.description: National Aeronautics and Space Administration leads U.S. space exploration, aeronautics research, and Earth science.
spendingItems: Artemis program — return to the Moon ($6B+/year)
Commercial crew (SpaceX, Boeing) and cargo contracts

examples: Boeing SLS (Space Launch System): $2.8B over original contract price
Orion spacecraft cost growth: $5.4B → $14.9B (276% increase)
Contractor labor mischarging on ISS operations

subCategories.name: National Science Foundation
subCategories.description: Funds basic research across all non-medical scientific disciplines at universities, supporting 300,000+ researchers annually.
spendingItems: Research grants in mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology
Major research infrastructure (telescopes, ice cores, ships)

examples: Foreign influence: researchers concealing Chinese government funding
Time and effort fraud — billing NSF for work done on other grants
Data fabrication in grant applications

subCategories.name: NOAA
subCategories.description: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration — weather forecasting, climate research, fisheries, and satellite operations.
spendingItems: National Weather Service (10,000 daily forecasts, warnings)

examples: Satellite contract overcharges
Fisheries permit fraud by fishing industry

name: Justice & Law Enforcement
description: Federal law enforcement, prosecution, courts, and corrections. Oversees FBI, DEA, ATF, federal prison system, and the federal judiciary.
spendingItems: FBI — counterterrorism, cybercrime, financial crime, violent crime
DEA — drug enforcement and international narcotics control
U.S. Marshals — fugitive apprehension and courthouse security
Federal judiciary — district and appellate courts
Grants to state and local law enforcement

examples: DOJ grant fraud — state and local recipients misusing federal law enforcement grants
BOP contractor billing fraud for inmate services
Asset forfeiture abuse and improper fund use
FBI/DEA informant payment fraud

subCategories.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation
subCategories.description: Primary federal law enforcement agency investigating terrorism, cybercrime, organized crime, public corruption, and civil rights violations.
spendingItems: Financial crime — securities fraud, money laundering
CJIS — criminal justice information services (fingerprints, NCIC)

examples: Undercover operation fund misuse
Confidential informant payment irregularities
IT project cost overruns (Sentinel system: $405M for $170M project)

subCategories.name: Bureau of Prisons
subCategories.description: Operates 122 federal correctional facilities housing 150,000 inmates and employing 35,000 staff.
spendingItems: Daily inmate housing and care ($44,000/inmate/year average)
Contracted private prison beds (CoreCivic, GEO Group)

examples: Private prison contractor overbilling for empty beds
Employee misconduct — smuggling contraband for payment
Healthcare contractor fraud — billing for unperformed services
FCI Dublin and other scandal-ridden facilities

name: Commerce & Trade
description: Department of Commerce programs supporting economic growth, trade promotion, census operations, and technology standards.
spendingItems: Census Bureau — decennial census and economic statistics
Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) — patent review and IP protection
NOAA (shared with Science) — weather and climate
CHIPS Act semiconductor grants ($52B program, multi-year)
Economic Development Administration — distressed community grants

examples: CHIPS Act grant fraud risk — semiconductor companies overstating eligibility
Census contractor billing fraud
EDA grant misuse by economic development organizations

subCategories.name: Patent & Trademark Office
subCategories.leadTitle: Under Secretary of Commerce for Intellectual Property
subCategories.description: Reviews and grants patents and trademarks, primarily self-funded by user fees.

examples: Patent examiner time fraud — telework abuse
Fraudulent patent applications from foreign entities

name: Labor & Workforce
description: Workplace safety, employment training, and worker protections. Note: unemployment insurance is counted under Income Security.
spendingItems: OSHA — workplace safety inspections and enforcement
Employment and Training Administration — job training grants
Bureau of Labor Statistics — CPI, jobs data, economic indicators
Wage and Hour Division — minimum wage and FMLA enforcement
Employee Benefits Security Administration — pension oversight

examples: Job training grant fraud — contractors billing for non-existent training
Davis-Bacon prevailing wage fraud on federal projects
Pension fund fraud by plan administrators

subCategories.name: Employment & Training Programs
subCategories.description: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) grants for job training, apprenticeships, and employment services.
spendingItems: Adult, dislocated worker, and youth job training
Trade Adjustment Assistance for displaced workers
Reentry programs for formerly incarcerated individuals

examples: Contractors billing for training participants who didn't attend
Inflated performance outcomes to maintain contracts

subCategories.leadTitle: Assistant Secretary for Occupational Safety & Health
subCategories.description: Sets and enforces workplace safety standards across 130M workers in 8M workplaces.
spendingItems: State plan programs (29 states run their own OSHA)
Standards development for new workplace hazards

examples: False safety certifications by contractors

name: Interior & Natural Resources
description: National parks, public lands, tribal programs, and natural resource management across 500M+ acres of federal land.
spendingItems: National Park Service — 423 parks, 330M annual visitors
Bureau of Land Management — 245M acres of public lands
U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service — endangered species, wildlife refuges
Bureau of Indian Affairs and Bureau of Indian Education
Bureau of Reclamation — western water infrastructure

examples: Oil and gas royalty underpayment on federal lands ($1B+/year estimated)
Tribal trust fund mismanagement (Cobell settlement: $3.4B)
Park concession contractor billing irregularities
Illegal mining and grazing on federal land

subCategories.name: National Park Service
subCategories.description: Manages 423 national parks, monuments, historic sites, and recreation areas — 85M acres protecting America's natural and cultural heritage.
spendingItems: Park operations: rangers, maintenance, visitor services

examples: Concession operator revenue underreporting
Construction contractor fraud

Law enforcement and detention on tribal lands
Social services: child welfare, housing, elderly care
Water rights settlements and land restoration

Contract support cost billing irregularities
School construction fraud at BIE schools

name: General Government & Administration
description: Congress, the White House, federal courts, and government-wide administrative agencies like GSA, OPM, and OMB.
spendingItems: Legislative Branch — Senate, House, support agencies ($7B)
Executive Office of the President — White House, NSC, OMB ($0.8B)
Federal court system — district, appeals, Supreme Court ($8B)
General Services Administration — federal buildings and purchasing
Office of Personnel Management — federal employee benefits

fraudEstimate.source: GAO High Risk List; GSA OIG; Judicial Conduct Reports
examples: GSA purchasing fraud and vendor kickbacks
Congressional office expenditure abuse ($5M+ in misused MRA funds)
Federal employee time fraud (telework era)
GSA Las Vegas conference scandal ($820K)

subCategories.name: Legislative Branch (Congress)

subCategories.description: Operating budget for the Senate, House of Representatives, Capitol Police, Library of Congress, CBO, GAO, and other legislative agencies.
spendingItems: Senate and House member offices and staff ($1.6B each)

examples: Member Representational Allowance (MRA) misuse — personal expenses charged
Insider trading by members on non-public information (STOCK Act violations)
Campaign fund commingling with office expenses

subCategories.name: Federal Judiciary
subCategories.description: Operating budgets for 94 district courts, 13 appellate courts, the Supreme Court, bankruptcy courts, and probation/pretrial services.
spendingItems: Federal judge salaries (1,000+ Article III judges)
Defender services — Federal Public Defender offices
Electronic filing and case management systems

examples: Recusal failures — judges hearing cases with financial conflicts
Interpreter fraud — billing for uncertified translations

subCategories.description: Government's landlord and buyer — manages 370M sq ft of federal real estate and $75B+ in annual government purchasing.
spendingItems: Technology acquisition and FedRAMP cloud authorization

Commercial item pricing manipulation
